title: Pre-op geriatric anesthesia documentation

medication.text: Supports previous anesthesia records and alerts plus age, comorbidity, medicines, allergy, surgery, pain, cognition, frailty, nutrition, lifestyle, psychological, functional, and chronic-pain risk assessment.
medication.rationale: The current RCoA section supports documenting age, comorbidity, cognition, frailty, nutrition, medicines, pain, lifestyle, and function in preoperative assessment without treating age as a diagnosis.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports previous anesthesia records and alerts plus age, comorbidity, medicines, allergy, surgery, pain, cognition, frailty, nutrition, lifestyle, psychological, functional, and chronic-pain risk assessment.

source.title: Guidelines for the Provision of Anaesthesia Services: Chapter 2 — Guidelines for the Provision of Anaesthesia Services for the Perioperative Care of Elective and Urgent Care Patients 2025

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 3.6 and 4.1–4.4 — previous records and risk domains

medication.text: Supports documenting frailty score, nutrition and BMI, comorbidities, cognition, medicines, functional status, reassessment, and individualized shared decisions.
medication.rationale: The frailty-specific section was reviewed but not mapped because geriatric age alone does not establish frailty.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting frailty score, nutrition and BMI, comorbidities, cognition, medicines, functional status, reassessment, and individualized shared decisions.

source.title: Guideline for Perioperative Care for People Living with Frailty Undergoing Elective and Emergency Surgery

exact_section.heading: Recommendations for primary care, surgical outpatients, and preoperative assessment

source_limitations: Age, baseline cognition, frailty status, function, falls, nutrition, multimorbidity, medicines, social support, capacity, goals, risk estimate, optimization, postoperative setting, and discharge needs remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
